title: 11 Herbal Teas For Toothache
intro_desc: Herbal teas have been used for centuries to relieve toothache pain. The active compounds in these plants help to reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause tooth infections. One example is ginger (Zingiber officinale), which contains compounds that have natural pain-relieving properties. Drinking ginger tea can help to numb the pain and reduce swelling.  Another herb that has been used to relieve toothache is Echinacea purpurea. This plant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the pain and swelling associated with tooth infections. Echinacea tea can also help to boost the immune system, which can help to fight off the underlying infection.  Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) is another herb that has been used to relieve toothache. The essential oils in lavender have a calming effect on the nerves, which can help to reduce pain and anxiety. Drinking lavender tea can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can be beneficial when dealing with toothache pain.  Drinking herbal teas for toothache can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help to reduce the need for over-the-counter pain medications, which can have negative side effects. Herbal teas are also a natural and non-invasive way to relieve toothache pain, which can be especially beneficial for people who are sensitive to medications. Additionally, using herbal teas for toothache can help to promote oral health and prevent future infections. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to maintaining good oral health and reducing the risk of toothache pain.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent toothache. Some herbal teas, like peppermint, have antibacterial properties that can help reduce plaque and fight gum disease, which can cause toothache. Others, like chamomile, can help soothe gum inflammation and ease pain. Drinking herbal tea regularly may help keep teeth and gums healthy.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent toothache. Some herbal teas have antibacterial properties that can reduce plaque and gum inflammation. This can help prevent tooth decay and gum disease, which are common causes of toothache. Regularly drinking these teas may help keep your teeth and gums healthy, but it's no substitute for regular brushing and flossing.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to ease a toothache, but drinking them every day may not be the best idea. Some herbs, like chamomile, can be safe to consume daily in small amounts, but others, like cloves, can be too strong and cause stomach problems if taken excessively.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like clove and peppermint can help with toothache. They may start to ease the pain within 15 to 30 minutes. This is because the active ingredients in these teas can reduce inflammation and numb the area. The pain relief can last for several hours, but it's best to drink the tea as soon as you feel the pain start.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for a toothache can be most effective when your body is most receptive to healing. This tends to be in the morning or early afternoon, when your body's natural energy levels are highest. At this time, your body is better equipped to absorb the beneficial compounds found in herbal teas, potentially providing faster relief from a toothache.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for toothache. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can slow down the action of certain painkillers. If you're taking prescription pain meds, it's best to talk to your pharmacist about any herbal teas you're drinking. This can help you avoid potential interactions and find a safe combination.
faq_pregnant: When it comes to herbal teas for toothache during pregnancy, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like clove and peppermint, might be safe, but others, like chamomile and ginger, could affect the baby's heart rate. To be on the safe side, try a small amount and observe how your body reacts.
faq_breastfeeding: When you have a toothache while breastfeeding, you might want to try herbal teas for relief. However, some herbal teas can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. Peppermint and chamomile teas are usually safe, but it's best to check the ingredients and choose a tea that's specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding mothers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate toothache pain. These compounds also have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and reduce swelling in the gum and mouth area. The warming sensation caused by the shogaols in Zingiber officinale teas can also help increase blood flow to the affected area, which can aid in healing and pain relief. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Zingiber officinale teas can help provide relief from toothache pain.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root (Zingiber officinale).
Peel the ginger root and grate it to release its juice.
Heat the water in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger root to the water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before drinking. Use 1/2 cup as needed for toothache relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with a toothache. The tea's flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial infections, a common cause of toothaches. The alkaloids in Echinacea purpurea, such as echinacoside, can also help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. By consuming Echinacea purpurea tea, you may experience relief from toothache pain due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down for 2-3 minutes before using it as a mouthwash.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the affected tooth for 10-15 minutes to help ease the pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the gums. The antiseptic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate can help combat the bacterial infection that often causes toothaches. The anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce pain and discomfort associated with toothaches. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Lavandula angustifolia teas can help alleviate symptoms of toothaches.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea slowly and regularly for relief from toothache.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like eucalyptol, a natural analgesic that helps reduce pain and inflammation. Eucalyptol also has antibacterial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that can cause toothaches. The antiseptic properties of eucalyptol help to reduce swelling and fight off germs that contribute to tooth infections. Eucalyptol's ability to numb the gums and teeth also helps to temporarily reduce pain and discomfort. By combining these properties, eucalyptus globulus teas can provide relief from toothache symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus leaves (Eucalyptus globulus) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of honey to the boiling water to make it sweeter.
Let the eucalyptus leaves steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the eucalyptus leaves.
Drink the eucalyptus tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve toothache pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and fight bacteria that may be causing the toothache. The sesquiterpene lactones in the tea, such as achillin and costunolide, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort. The flavonoids and terpenoids in the tea also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the teeth and gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, Achillea millefolium teas may help alleviate toothache symptoms and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea if desired, and stir well.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed for toothache relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas contains salvenolic acids and tannins as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight infection in the gums. The tannins in Salvia officinalis teas also have astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and ease pain by constricting blood vessels. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis teas can help combat the bacteria that cause tooth infections, providing relief from toothache. By reducing inflammation and fighting infection, Salvia officinalis teas can help alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into another cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for flavor.
Drink the tea slowly to help ease the toothache.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is also known as glycyrrhetic acid. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth. The tea also contains flavonoids, which are antioxidants that help to fight off bacteria and viruses that can cause tooth infections. Glycyrrhizin has also been shown to have antiseptic properties, which help to kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause toothache. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help to alleviate toothache pain.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and a tea infuser.
Boil the 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add the 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the tea infuser.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using it to relieve toothache.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum teas contains a compound called eugenol, which is a natural pain reliever. Eugenol blocks the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. The tea also contains caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and ease toothache pain. The combination of eugenol and caryophyllene in Syzygium aromaticum teas makes it a natural alternative for treating toothache pain. When consumed, these compounds work together to provide relief from toothache pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain signals.
remedies.image_alt: Syzygium aromaticum herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Measure out 3-4 whole cloves of Syzygium aromaticum and place them in a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the cloves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste.
Drink the tea slowly and carefully, allowing the cloves to ease the toothache pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa teas contains active constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain. Curcumin also has analgesic properties that directly target pain receptors, providing relief from toothache. Additionally, curcuma longa teas has antimicrobial properties that prevent bacterial growth, which can cause infections and exacerbate toothache. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Curcuma longa teas can help alleviate toothache symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Curcuma longa root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for a sweeter taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve toothache pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the mouth, which can cause toothache. Rosmarinus officinalis also contains carnosic acid, a compound that has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the gums and teeth from damage. The tea's antimicrobial properties can help to fight off infections that can cause toothache, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce pain and discomfort. The combination of these compounds in Rosmarinus officinalis teas makes it a potential remedy for toothache relief.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using as a mouthwash or drink 1/4 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for toothache relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to reduce swelling and ease pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Cinnamomum verum teas also contains eugenol, a natural analgesic that numbs the area and provides relief from toothache pain. The antiseptic properties of Cinnamomum verum teas, due to its eugenol content, help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation in the gums. By consuming Cinnamomum verum teas, you can take advantage of its natural pain-relieving properties to find relief from toothache.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal teas for toothache
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Cinnamomum verum bark, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a pot.
Add the Cinnamomum verum bark to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using it to treat toothache.
